title: Order of 19 September 2025 — Kohler and Paris (France v. Islamic Republic of Iran) (Discontinuance)
text:
19 SEPTEMBER 2025 
ORDER 
KOHLER AND PARIS  
(FRANCE v. ISLAMIC REPUBLIC OF IRAN) 
___________ 
KOHLER ET PARIS  
(FRANCE c. RÉPUBLIQUE ISLAMIQUE D’IRAN) 
19 SEPTEMBRE 2025 
ORDONNANCE 

INTERNATIONAL COURT OF JUSTICE 
YEAR 2025 
2025 
19 September 
General List 
No. 199 
19 September 2025 
KOHLER AND PARIS 
(FRANCE v. ISLAMIC REPUBLIC OF IRAN) 
ORDER 
Present: 
President IWASAWA; Vice-President SEBUTINDE; Judges TOMKA, XUE, NOLTE, 
CHARLESWORTH, 
GÓMEZ 
ROBLEDO, 
CLEVELAND, 
AURESCU, 
HMOUD; 
Registrar GAUTIER. 
The International Court of Justice, 
Composed as above, 
After deliberation, 
Having regard to Article 48 of the Statute of the Court and to Article 89, paragraph 2, of the 
Rules of Court, 
Having regard to the Application filed in the Registry of the Court on 16 May 2025, whereby 
the French Republic (hereinafter “France”) instituted proceedings against the Islamic Republic of 
Iran (hereinafter “Iran”) with regard to a dispute concerning alleged violations by the latter of its 
obligations under Article 36 of the Vienna Convention on Consular Relations of 24 April 1963 in the 
context of the arrest, detention and trial of two French nationals in Iran, 

- 2 -
Having regard to the Order of 17 July 2025, whereby the Court fixed 2 December 2025 and 
17 April 2026 as the respective time-limits for the filing of the Memorial of France and the 
Counter-Memorial of Iran; 
Whereas, by a letter dated 15 September 2025 and received in the Registry on the same day, 
the Agent of France, referring to Article 89 of the Rules of Court, informed the Court that his 
Government wished to discontinue the proceedings; 
Whereas a copy of that letter was immediately communicated to the Government of Iran, 
which was informed that 23 September 2025 had been fixed as the time‑limit provided for in 
Article 89, paragraph 2, of the Rules, within which Iran could state whether it opposed the 
discontinuance; 
Whereas, by a letter dated 17 September 2025 and received in the Registry on the same day, 
the Agent of Iran informed the Court that his Government had no objection to the discontinuance of 
the proceedings as requested by France, 
Places on record the discontinuance by the French Republic of the proceedings instituted by 
its Application filed on 16 May 2025; and 
Directs that the case be removed from the List. 
Done in English and in French, the English text being authoritative, at the Peace Palace, 
The Hague, this nineteenth day of September, two thousand and twenty-five, in three copies, one of 
which will be placed in the archives of the Court and the others transmitted to the Government of the 
French Republic and the Government of the Islamic Republic of Iran, respectively. 
(Signed) 
IWASAWA Yuji, 
President. 
(Signed) 
Philippe GAUTIER, 
Registrar. 
___________
case_name: Kohler and Paris (France v. Islamic Republic of Iran)
